codeReview.summary: The commit is narrowly scoped to `client/tests/test_tui.py` and replaces an `AsyncMock` patch on `CCSentimentApp._poll_card` with a synchronous `Mock` callback, matching the feature goal of removing unawaited coroutine warnings without changing TUI source behavior.

sharedStateObservations.observation: Worker deviated from the baseline-validation stop procedure when the baseline failure was the exact in-scope bug this feature was assigned to fix; skill guidance may need an explicit exception for this case.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Handoff `skillFeedback.followedProcedure=false` with deviation at `1.3 Baseline Validation` and suggested change to allow continuation for explicitly assigned in-scope failures; transcript skeleton shows baseline `uv run pytest -xvs` followed by direct diagnosis and fix.

sharedStateObservations.observation: Mission shared guidance still describes the AsyncMock warning issue as a pre-existing baseline problem even though this feature now resolves it.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: /Users/yasyf/.factory/missions/c3c5e3fe-2fb5-4733-9f3a-5ae91c0f3321/AGENTS.md:108 lists the AsyncMock RuntimeWarnings as pre-existing and tracked by this feature; commit `aad8c97ad762828fadfcd671d3e7b134133a77a5` fixes the relevant fixture.

summary: Pass. The reviewed diff directly addresses the AsyncMock misuse in existing TUI tests and remains within feature scope. Evidence in the handoff indicates full-suite verification passed with RuntimeWarnings promoted to errors and no production-source changes.
